expectations.text: Reports findings in a structured format

validate_output.txt confirms: 'semgrep validate /home/oat/.config/opencode/.semgrep/my-rules.yaml' — result: 'Configuration is valid - found 0 fatal errors, 0 skippable error(s), and 3 rule(s).'

scan_output.txt shows scan with 3 Code rules (matching the 3 custom rules). Command: 'semgrep scan --config /home/oat/.config/opencode/.semgrep/my-rules.yaml --json --output results.json --error src/'
Explicitly uses '--json' flag in the scan command as shown above

expectations.text: Uses --output or --json-output to save to results.json

Command includes '--error' flag. Exit code 1 (findings detected, strict mode blocking the pipeline)
Command targets 'src/' as the scan path. Scan output confirms: 'Scanning 1 file tracked by git' (the src/example.py file)

Terminal output confirms: 'Scanning 0 files tracked by git with 1059 Code rules' and 'Skipping baseline scan, because there are no current findings.' The --baseline-commit flag is documented in the command summary
Command uses '--config p/default'. Terminal confirms 1059 Code rules loaded from community registry
Command uses '--sarif' and '--sarif-output outputs/results.sarif'. SARIF file generated at 2,075,615 bytes with valid v2.1.0 JSON
Command includes '--error' flag. Exit code 0 (no findings, passed cleanly)
